hero.title: Hepius Therapeutics
hero.subtitle: Next-Generation Precision Immuno-Oncology
hero.tagline: Translating patient-centric biology into next-generation precision immuno-oncology therapeutics — beyond PD-1.

cards.title: Reverse Translational Platform
cards.desc: A patient-centric, biomarker-driven platform that integrates clinical data with laboratory discovery to identify targets, select indications, and guide drug development from bedside to bench and back.

cards.title: TCR Discovery & Engineering
cards.desc: A 4–8-week TCR discovery workflow, combined with proprietary soluble TCR expression and cell-based affinity screening, enabling targeting of intracellular antigens.

cards.title: Multi-Modality Therapeutics
cards.desc: Spanning antibodies, TCR-CD3 bispecifics, ADCs, and radiopharmaceuticals (RDC) — matching each target and indication with the optimal therapeutic modality.

about.mission: While PD-1 inhibitors have transformed cancer treatment, many patients remain unresponsive or develop resistance. Hepius Therapeutics is built on a reverse translational medicine platform that starts from patient data — identifying resistance mechanisms and novel targets to develop next-generation precision immuno-oncology therapeutics for unmet clinical needs.

overview.approachDesc: We operate a dual-engine model — advancing core programs in-house while building strategic partnerships with global pharma. We pursue out-licensing for select programs at the right inflection points, ensuring our therapies reach patients as efficiently as possible.

science.pageSubtitle: A patient-centric reverse translational platform powered by proprietary TCR discovery and engineering capabilities.

platform.title: Reverse Translational Platform
platform.desc: Hepius's reverse translational platform integrates clinical data analysis with patient-derived sample profiling. Two pillars work together to identify therapeutic targets and screen drug candidates against patients most likely to respond.

pillar1.title: Clinical Bioinformatics-Guided Target Selection
bullets: Collaborate with leading clinical institutions to identify novel targets
Analyze patient cohorts receiving immunotherapy to identify PD-1i resistant/refractory mechanisms
Biomarker-driven indication selection and patient stratification

pillar2.title: Patient Tissue Bank-Based Drug Screening
bullets: Patient-derived PBMC, DTC, and TIL libraries
In-house immune profiling with multi-color flow cytometry

cards.title: 4–8-Week TCR Discovery Pipeline
cards.desc: Identification of antigen-specific native TCRs from PBMCs/TILs within four to eight weeks via tetramer staining, FACS sorting, and 10X single-cell sequencing.

cards.title: Soluble TCR Expression
cards.desc: Engineered constant-region mutations and Fc fusion enable yields up to 93 mg/L for native TCRs without affinity enhancement.

cards.title: Cell-Based TCR Affinity Screening
cards.desc: Proprietary THP-1 cell-based screening method enables affinity characterization of soluble native TCRs — solving a long-standing challenge in preclinical TCR research.

cards.title: Multi-Format TCR Therapeutics
cards.desc: Versatile molecular formats — monovalent scTCR, bivalent TCR-Fc, and TCR-CD3 T-cell engagers — supporting both therapeutic and radiodiagnostic applications.

pipeline.pageSubtitle: Differentiated programs across multiple modalities — antibodies, TCR-CD3 T-cell engagers, and radioconjugates.

hp001.title: A Differentiated Monoclonal Antibody for Combination with PD-1 Inhibitors
hp001.desc: HP-001 is a humanized monoclonal antibody directed against a novel immunosuppressive pathway, designed for combination use with PD-1 inhibitors to broaden the population of patients benefiting from immune checkpoint therapy. Informed by insights from Hepius's reverse translational platform — which characterizes resistance mechanisms in patients exposed to PD-1 blockade — HP-001 carries a differentiated scientific rationale and positioning. The lead candidate has completed antibody discovery, humanization, and in vitro / in vivo activity evaluation, and is advancing through preclinical candidate (PCC) work.
highlights: Humanized monoclonal antibody against a novel immunosuppressive pathway
Designed for combination with PD-1 inhibitors in PD-1i resistant / refractory solid tumors
Antibody discovery, humanization, and in vitro / in vivo efficacy completed

hp002.title: PRAME TCR Platform: Dual Modality Therapy & Diagnostics
facts: Platform: Native soluble TCR, no affinity enhancement
hp002.desc: HP-002 is a soluble native T-cell receptor specifically recognizing PRAME₄₂₅₋₄₃₃ in complex with HLA-A*02:01. Built on Hepius's proprietary TCR discovery and engineering platform, HP-002 is being developed in two parallel modalities: a TCR-CD3 bispecific T-cell engager (TCE) for therapy, and a radiolabeled diagnostic agent for SPECT/CT imaging.
hp002.affinityNote: Measured by SPR against PRAME₄₂₅₋₄₃₃/HLA-A*02:01 monomer.
hp002.sprCaption: Figure: SPR binding curves of bivalent PR2-Fc TCR to immobilized PRAME₄₂₅₋₄₃₃/HLA-A*02:01 monomer at 8 concentrations (0.78–100 μg/ml).
hp002.therapyDesc: Three TCR-CD3 bispecific formats (002BsAb01/02/03) engineered with varying CD3 engagement geometry and valency. The lead candidate fully activates T cells in vitro and induces potent antigen-specific killing of PRAME-positive Hs695T tumor cells — without requiring TCR affinity enhancement.
hp002.diagDesc: ¹²⁵I-labeled PR2-Fc-LALAPG variant demonstrates antigen-dependent tumor accumulation in PRAME-positive Hs695T xenograft models, with peak uptake at ~24 hours post-injection. Single-chain TCR variant (scPRC2) showed faster tumor uptake at ~4 hours with rapid clearance.
hp002.spectCaption: Figure 7 (AACR 2026 Poster #7193): ¹²⁵I-labeled native PRAME-specific TCR exhibited tumor accumulation in Hs695T xenograft model. (A) Bivalent PR2-Fc-LALAPG showed peak tumor uptake at ~24 h post-injection. (B) Single-chain scPRC2 exhibited highest tumor accumulation at ~4 h with faster clearance. Red circles indicate tumor regions.
hp002.extDesc: The same TCR discovery platform has identified MAGE-A4₂₃₀₋₂₃₉/HLA-A*02:01-specific TCRs (KD = 9.18 × 10⁻⁷ M), expanding the pipeline to additional HLA-restricted intracellular tumor antigens.
hp002.refText: Detailed data presented at AACR Annual Meeting 2026, Poster #7193.

hp004.title: A Dual-Modality Platform Targeting ALPP/ALPPL2 (ADC + TCE)
hp004.desc: HP-004 is a platform spanning antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) and bispecific T-cell engager (TCE) modalities, targeting ALPP and ALPPL2 — alkaline phosphatase isozymes highly expressed in ovarian, endometrial, gastric, and non-small cell lung cancers, with restricted expression in normal tissues. HP-004 antibodies specifically bind ALPP/ALPPL2 without cross-reactivity to ALPI or ALPL, minimizing on-target off-tumor liability. Lead candidate: HY-X7514 (data presented at AACR 2026).
highlights: IC₅₀ ~248 pM in ALPP/ALPPL2-overexpressing tumor cell lines
Enhanced internalization efficiency over reference antibodies (Seagen)
Near-complete tumor regression at 3 mg/kg (BiW × 2) in NCI-H1651 xenograft model
Complete tumor regression in 6 of 8 treated mice
Bystander killing activity in ALPP/ALPPL2-positive / ALPP/ALPPL2-negative co-culture models
hp004.refText: Detailed data presented at AACR Annual Meeting 2026, Poster #6776.

ip.title: Intellectual Property
ip.desc: Hepius has filed multiple invention patents (China + US) covering its core programs and platform technologies. Detailed IP information available upon request through BD.

publications.pageSubtitle: Peer-reviewed presentations of our scientific progress.

posters.title: Utilize a Soluble T-cell Receptor (TCR) Targeting Tumor Intracellular Antigen PRAME to Develop Therapeutic and Radioactive Diagnostic Agents for PRAME-positive Tumors
posters.authors: Xiao Liang, Yajuan Xue, Yujun Huang
posters.abstract: We established a proprietary TCR discovery platform for rapid identification of PRAME₄₂₅₋₄₃₃/HLA-A*02:01-specific TCRs without affinity enhancement. The lead candidate HP-002 (KD = 1.08 × 10⁻⁷ M) demonstrates dual-modality potential: TCR-CD3 bispecific T-cell engagers for therapy and ¹²⁵I-labeled radiotracers for SPECT/CT diagnostics. ¹²⁵I-PR2-Fc-LALAPG showed antigen-dependent tumor accumulation in Hs695T xenograft models.

posters.title: HP004, a Novel Antibody-Drug Conjugate Targeting Alkaline Phosphatases ALPP and ALPPL2 in Solid Tumors
posters.authors: Xiao Liang, Yajuan Xue, Yujun Huang
posters.abstract: ALPP and ALPPL2 are highly expressed in ovarian, endometrial, gastric, and non-small cell lung cancers, with restricted expression in normal tissues. HP-004 is a novel ADC dual-targeting ALPP/ALPPL2, conjugated with MMAE at an average DAR of ~4, demonstrating high selectivity and enhanced internalization with an IC₅₀ of ~248 pM. In the NCI-H1651 xenograft lung cancer model, HP-004 achieved near-complete tumor regression at 3 mg/kg (BiW × 2), with complete tumor regression in 6 of 8 mice.

publications.comingSoon: More publications and presentations to follow.
